quick_facts.label: Service and entry post
quick_facts.value: Indian Telecommunication Service (ITS) Group ‘A’ officers appointed through the Engineering Services Examination enter as Assistant Divisional Engineer Telecom in the Junior Time Scale (JTS).

quick_facts.label: Examination stream
quick_facts.value: In the UPSC Engineering Services Examination notice, Indian Telecommunication Service Group ‘A’ is listed under Category IV: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering.

quick_facts.label: Cadre-controlling context
quick_facts.value: The Department of Telecommunications (DoT), Ministry of Communications, administers the service; its functions include telecom-policy implementation, licensing, radio-frequency management, and wireless-regulatory enforcement.

quick_facts.label: Initial training
quick_facts.value: NCA-T, Ghaziabad (formerly NTIPRIT) conducts induction training for candidates joining JTS in ITS Group A.

quick_facts.label: Entry pay context
quick_facts.value: A DoT appointment offer dated July 17, 2025 placed Assistant Divisional Engineer Telecom (JTS of ITS Group ‘A’) at Level 10 in the 7th Central Pay Commission matrix: ₹56,100–₹177,500 plus applicable allowances. This is dated context, not a guarantee of current or future take-home pay.

sections.heading: What ITS officers do
paragraphs: ITS is a technical civil service within DoT. The work sits at the junction of engineering, public administration, and telecom regulation: DoT formulates and implements telecommunications policy, grants telecom-service licences, manages radio frequencies, and monitors wireless transmissions for regulatory enforcement.

The official DoT induction material identifies the operating domains in which ITS probationers are prepared: telecom technologies; telecom enforcement and resource monitoring; licensing; standards development; and Universal Service Obligation work. The precise assignment varies by posting and grade rather than being a single fixed job description.
bullets: Support implementation of government telecom policy and licence-related administration.
Work on technical and regulatory functions involving networks, spectrum/radio communications, standards, monitoring, enforcement, or universal-service programmes.
Translate technical analysis into administrative proposals, compliance processes, coordination with licensees and other public bodies, and field-level implementation.

sections.heading: Primary route: UPSC Engineering Services Examination
paragraphs: The normal direct-recruitment route evidenced by DoT appointment orders is the UPSC Engineering Services Examination (ESE/IES), followed by service allocation and appointment to Assistant Divisional Engineer Telecom, JTS of ITS Group ‘A’. Allocation is based on examination merit, the candidate’s service preferences, vacancies, and medical fitness under the applicable examination rules.

For the ESE 2025 notice, ITS was in the Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering category. That notice required an engineering degree or another listed equivalent qualification, and its process included Preliminary (Stage I), Main (Stage II), and Personality Test (Stage III). Eligibility rules, vacancies, dates, fees, age limits, medical standards, and category provisions can change by examination cycle, so applicants should use the UPSC notice for the year in which they apply.
bullets: Build eligibility around an engineering qualification accepted in the relevant UPSC ESE notice.
Apply specifically in the Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering stream when ITS is listed for that cycle.
Prepare for the written stages and Personality Test; final service allocation also depends on rank, preferences, vacancies, and medical fitness.
After appointment, expect a two-year probation period under the cited 2025 DoT offer, subject to applicable government rules.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: The role requires both engineering depth and the ability to operate in a government-policy environment. DoT’s training material explicitly covers telecom technologies, licensing, standards development, telecom enforcement and resource monitoring, and the management and administrative dimensions of the telecom sector.

Candidates benefit from strong Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering foundations because the UPSC ESE places ITS in that discipline. NCA-T’s current capacity-building portfolio also points to contemporary areas relevant to the service, including 5G, telecom security, AI, IoT, and green-energy applications.
bullets: Electronics and telecommunication engineering fundamentals, including networks and wireless communications.
Technical reading, standards awareness, and evidence-based analysis for licensing, compliance, and policy implementation.
Understanding of spectrum/resource monitoring and telecom-security concepts.
Clear official writing, stakeholder coordination, and administrative judgement.
Continuous learning: DoT runs career-linked mid-career training for ITS Group ‘A’ officers before higher-responsibility roles.

sections.heading: Where officers may work
paragraphs: Postings are not limited to one office type. DoT’s induction material describes 22 Licensed Service Areas (LSAs), whose field units represent the licensor/telegraph authority and report to the Director General Telecom. Official personnel records also identify ITS officers across DoT headquarters, DGT/LSAs, the Telecommunication Engineering Centre (TEC), National Centre for Communication Security (NCCS), NCA-T, and, in relevant cases, BSNL and MTNL.

Accordingly, the work can combine headquarters policy or programme work with technical/regulatory field responsibilities, specialised standards or security assignments, training/research functions, and transfers or deputation as provided under service rules.
bullets: DoT headquarters and Directorate General Telecom organisations.
Licensed Service Area field offices.
Specialised DoT institutions such as TEC, NCCS, and NCA-T.
Potential assignments connected with public-sector telecom organisations, subject to service and posting orders.

sections.heading: Career development and progression
paragraphs: The documented entry grade is JTS. DoT publishes seniority and promotion material for higher ITS Group ‘A’ levels, including Senior Time Scale (STS), Junior Administrative Grade (JAG), Higher Administrative Grade (HAG), and HAG+ (Advisor) postings. Advancement is therefore structured within a graded civil-service cadre, but timing and individual outcomes depend on the applicable rules, vacancies, assessment, eligibility, and government decisions.

Learning remains part of progression. A DoT order dated June 30, 2026 describes Mid-Career Training Programme participation for ITS Group ‘A’ officers as career-linked mandatory training before promotion to higher-responsibility positions.
bullets: Start as Assistant Divisional Engineer Telecom in JTS.
Complete probation and induction training, then develop operational, regulatory, policy, and management capability through assignments.
Expect formal seniority, promotion, and posting processes rather than an automatic private-sector-style promotion schedule.
Complete required mid-career learning as prescribed for career progression.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: The most directly evidenced entry-pay reference located is DoT’s July 17, 2025 offer for Assistant Divisional Engineer Telecom (JTS of ITS Group ‘A’): Level 10, ₹56,100–₹177,500 in the 7th Central Pay Commission pay matrix, plus allowances. This is the basic-pay matrix range cited in that order; it should not be read as a current net-salary quote.

Actual monthly remuneration can vary with the prevailing central-government allowance orders, posting location, deductions, and any future pay-policy revisions. Candidates should verify the appointment offer and current Department of Expenditure/DoT instructions applicable on their date of joining.
bullets: Use the cited Level 10 figure as historical appointment-order context dated July 17, 2025.
Distinguish basic pay from gross and take-home pay, which depend on allowances and deductions.
Do not rely on coaching-site salary estimates in place of the applicable government appointment and pay orders.

useful_links.label: UPSC ESE 2025 notification (official reference for ITS stream and process)
